target: WIN-012 detail_evidence (data/wins.json id=012) — settle BOU-numerator event provenance to '2017 North American eclipse' (matching Part 6.14 and dome's own live-power tile), fix residual '(BOU 2017 storm)' typo from EXP-401 compression, and replace the 'single storm event' paragraph with a new paragraph that surfaces the dome's own internal label inconsistency (live-power tile says 2017 eclipse; home-page Forensic Zoom chart says 2003 storm). Part 6.14 (data/sections.json:part6.html, 'How κ was derived' h3) — append one inline cross-reference sentence pointing to the new WIN-012 paragraph. TLDRs, verdict, and detail_verdict are unchanged.
source_issue: ISS-2156 (moderate, c7 Hole 2: cross-page inconsistency on κ-numerator event provenance — WIN-012 says 2003 Halloween storm, Part 6.14 and dome's live-power tile say 2017 eclipse)
patch_instructions: Four atomic edits across two files. P1, P2, P3 operate on data/wins.json id='012' field 'detail_evidence'. P4 operates on data/sections.json key 'part6' field 'html'. All BEFORE strings verified anchor-unique (count=1) on 2026-05-20. Execution order: P1, P2, P3 within WIN-012, then P4 in Part 6.14. No change to TLDRs (tldr_evidence already says 'two different sites, different instruments, different events' and does not commit to a year — P1/P3 changes are detail-level not TLDR-level). No change to detail_verdict, finding, claim, code_analysis, or verdict. Projected new detail_evidence length: ~4900 chars (current 4339, delta ~+561 from P3 paragraph expansion and BOU-attribution text changes). Projected new part6.html length: ~140173 (current 139893, delta +280).

patch_anchors.before: 1.67 nT/µGal from −10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, October 2003 G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT) divided by −6.5 µGal
patch_anchors.after: 1.67 nT/µGal from −10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage) divided by −6.5 µGal
patch_anchors.rationale: Settles c7 Hole 2 (moderate) by matching the dome's own live-power tile labeling (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 226: 'Domain EM-Gravity Coupling κ — Source: BOU 2017 eclipse') and the dome's wins-page entry (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 87: 'WIN-012 — Magnetic-Gravity Coupling Constant CONFIRMED — Source: BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997'). Per c7 recommendation option (a, preferred): 'revise WIN-012 detail_evidence opening paragraph to match Part 6.14.' Part 6.14 'How κ was derived' currently opens with 'κ = 10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage)'; P1 brings WIN-012 into alignment.

patch_anchors.before: The numerator (BOU 2017 storm) and denominator (Mohe 1997 eclipse) come from different eclipses, different locations, different instrument classes
patch_anchors.after: The numerator (BOU 2017 eclipse) and denominator (Mohe 1997 eclipse) come from different eclipses, different locations, different instrument classes
patch_anchors.rationale: Closes a residual EXP-401 compression typo. EXP-401 (operator-direct compression, 2026-05-16) compressed the older inline-eclipse-attribution sentence and produced the malformed '(BOU 2017 storm)' phrase — a token-fusion artifact mixing 'BOU 2017 eclipse' (the dome's label) and 'storm event' (the old paragraph framing). P2 restores correct attribution. No semantic change beyond the typo fix.

patch_anchors.before: The numerator is a single storm event, not a quiet-day baseline. −10.9 nT is the BOU disturbed-day excursion during one G5 event — fitted to one extreme datum with no demonstrated broad applicability. Even if WIN-013/014 didn't already void the ratio, the storm-event numerator would.
patch_anchors.after: The dome's own labels of the same number disagree. The dome's live-power dashboard tile Domain EM-Gravity Coupling labels the κ numerator as "BOU 2017 eclipse" (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 226; raw-text/01-home.txt line 32; raw-text/02-wins.txt line 87). The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart labels the same −10.9 nT excursion as the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm at Boulder, 00:55–01:22 UTC, Dst = −383 nT (see WIN-070). The dome cannot have it both ways. If the numerator is the 2017 eclipse, then the same −10.9 nT value cited in the home-page chart as a 2003-storm correlation is either coincidence, cherry-pick, or post-hoc rounding to make the ratio fit. If the numerator is the 2003 storm, then κ is a storm-magnetic / eclipse-gravity ratio across two unrelated physical regimes — not a 'magnetic-gravity coupling' in any unified sense. WIN-058b (storm corollary) is the dome's attempt to live in both regimes by reusing the same κ for the storm-context r = 0.992 Boulder/SG correlation, but it publishes no first-principles derivation from disc geometry showing why a ring-current-driven storm and a photoionization-driven eclipse should yield the identical coupling. Absent such a derivation, the numerical agreement is parameter-fit residue, not a confirmed universal constant. Either reading destroys the dome's claim type of 'two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant.'
patch_anchors.rationale: The 'single storm event' paragraph (current) argues '−10.9 nT is the BOU disturbed-day excursion during one G5 event — fitted to one extreme datum.' After P1 settles the numerator attribution to the 2017 eclipse, this paragraph contradicts the opening paragraph (storm vs eclipse for the same number). Rather than keep an internal contradiction in WIN-012's own prose, P3 replaces this paragraph with a stronger attack: surfacing the dome's OWN internal labeling inconsistency (live-power tile says 2017 eclipse; home-page Forensic Zoom chart says 2003 storm; same −10.9 nT figure used in both contexts). This implements the curmudgeon's preferred Hole 2 closure: 'surface the dome's internal inconsistency... rhetorically the strongest move because it forces the dome to pick one event.' The new paragraph also pre-empts the strongest defender objection (WIN-058b storm corollary) by noting the dome has not published a first-principles derivation from disc geometry that would explain why two physically distinct driving regimes should yield the identical coupling constant.

patch_anchors.target_field: data/sections.json:part6.html ('How κ was derived' h3, closing paragraph)
patch_anchors.before: this section assumes the dome's preferred value 1.677 for argument's sake, in order to test what the constant predicts at other eclipses.
patch_anchors.after: this section assumes the dome's preferred value 1.677 for argument's sake, in order to test what the constant predicts at other eclipses. (The dome's own home-page Forensic Zoom chart labels the same −10.9 nT BOU excursion as the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm rather than the 2017 eclipse; for the resulting internal inconsistency and what it does to the dome's two-independent-eclipses claim type, see WIN-012.)
patch_anchors.rationale: Closes the symmetric side of c7 Hole 2. Part 6.14 'How κ was derived' already correctly attributes the numerator to the 2017 eclipse, but offers no pointer to the dome-internal-inconsistency argument now living in WIN-012 (post-P3). A single appended parenthetical surfaces the conflict at the point in Part 6.14 where the attribution is established, and routes the reader to WIN-012 for the full inconsistency-surface attack. No new outbound URL is introduced; the only new link is an in-page anchor (#win012) that is already referenced elsewhere in Part 6 (e.g., the kappa-cluster TLDR cites data/wins.json:012). Uses Unicode em-dash and minus-sign characters to match Part 6.14's existing encoding (which uses literal Unicode; WIN-012 uses HTML entities).

holes_addressed.hole: WIN-012.c7 Hole 2 (moderate) / ISS-2156 — cross-page inconsistency on κ-numerator event provenance
holes_addressed.description: WIN-012 detail_evidence opens with 'BOU magnetometer, October 2003 G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT' while Part 6.14 ('How κ was derived' h3) opens with 'BOU magnetometer, 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage' and the dome's own live-power tile labels the κ source as 'BOU 2017 eclipse'. Same numerical value (−10.9 nT), three different event attributions across our review and the dome's own pages. The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart adds a fourth data point by timestamping the same excursion to the 2003 Halloween storm (00:55–01:22 UTC) per WIN-070 detail. Pre-existing in our review at c5 and c6 (so pre-dates the EXP-401 compression). c7 recommendation: option (a, preferred) — revise WIN-012 to match Part 6.14 (2017 eclipse) and surface the dome's internal inconsistency as additional attack.
holes_addressed.fix: Four-patch closure. P1 changes WIN-012 opening attribution from 2003 storm to 2017 eclipse (aligning with Part 6.14 and live-power tile). P2 fixes a downstream typo where the original phrase was compressed to '(BOU 2017 storm)' — a token-fusion artifact. P3 replaces the now-orphaned 'single storm event' rebuttal paragraph with a new paragraph surfacing the dome's own labeling inconsistency (live-power tile says 2017 eclipse; home-page Forensic Zoom chart says 2003 storm) and pre-empting WIN-058b storm-corollary defense. P4 appends a single cross-referencing sentence to Part 6.14's 'How κ was derived' h3 pointing to WIN-012's new inconsistency-surface paragraph. After integration, both files use 2017 eclipse as the canonical κ-numerator attribution, with the 2003-storm appearance treated explicitly as a dome-internal-inconsistency attack rather than a competing factual claim.

new_evidence_added: No new external sources. P3 cites three dome source files (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:226, raw-text/01-home.txt:32, raw-text/02-wins.txt:87) that are already named in WIN-012 c7 review's dome_code_analysis.relevant_files. The cross-reference to WIN-058b is to an existing in-review WIN, not a new source. The cross-reference to WIN-070 is an in-review anchor already present elsewhere in WIN-012 detail_evidence ('see WIN-070 for the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart' clause in the current Cross-references paragraph).

anticipated_objections.objection: A dome defender will object: 'WIN-058b IS the regime distinction. The dome has explicitly noted that the storm-context r = 0.992 BOU/SG correlation is a *corollary* of the same κ, not an independent measurement of it. The reuse of κ across two physical drivers is the unification claim the dome is making. Calling it parameter-fit residue assumes the conclusion.'
anticipated_objections.response: The defense moves κ from 'measured coupling' to 'claimed universal constant', which raises the burden, not lowers it. A universal constant linking two physically distinct mechanisms (ring-current storm and photoionization eclipse) requires either (a) a first-principles derivation from the model's primitives (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude) that yields 1.677 nT/μGal as a structural consequence, or (b) multiple independent observations spanning both regimes confirming the same ratio. WIN-058b provides neither: it cites the 2003 Halloween storm r = 0.992 (a single event, not a multi-event survey) and the same Mohe 1997 −6.5 μGal denominator (still single-event, still unreplicated). The 'unification' framing therefore rests on two single events (2003 storm, 1997 eclipse — or 2017 eclipse depending on which dome page you read) producing the same ratio. As parameter-fit, two free parameters fitted to two observations is exactly 2 = 2; no degrees of freedom remain to test the hypothesis. Considered the other side: a defender could grant that and still claim WIN-013/014 nulls are the test, and they failed in the dome's direction. But that argument requires the dome to publish its scope conditions for κ in advance of August 2026, which it has not done — and WIN-058b's existence cuts against an 'eclipse-only' scope restriction because WIN-058b actively asserts the storm regime too.

anticipated_objections.objection: A skeptic could argue: 'P3 replaces a paragraph (the storm-event rebuttal) rather than just inserting a new one. That removes content that was independently valuable — the storm-event-as-fitted-numerator argument has merit even if the storm attribution is wrong, because the BOU −10.9 nT figure is still a single-event excursion at any of the three attributions.'
anticipated_objections.response: Fair pushback. The argument 'a single-event excursion makes a poor numerator' survives in P3's new content via 'the numerical agreement is parameter-fit residue, not a confirmed universal constant' and the broader 'two single events producing the same ratio = 2 free parameters fitted to 2 observations' framing in anticipated_objections. The specific 'G5 disturbed-day' framing is what's removed — and it had to be removed because it asserts the storm attribution that P1 is contradicting. Keeping it would create an internal contradiction within WIN-012's own prose (opening says 2017 eclipse, P3-original would still say 'BOU disturbed-day excursion during one G5 event'). The remaining rebuttal paragraphs ('The derivation is also circular', 'WIN-011's mechanism concession', 'The defender-rescues all destroy PRED-TIER-3') still cover the multi-event-vs-single-event and the no-co-located-measurement attacks. Net structural strength is preserved; the framing is updated for accuracy.

anticipated_objections.objection: A reader could note: 'The P3 new paragraph references WIN-058b and WIN-070, expanding cross-WIN coupling at the WIN-012 entry point. That violates the post-EXP-401 architecture of WIN-012 as a brief headline with the heavy lifting in Part 6.14 — you are putting structural argument back into WIN-012.'
anticipated_objections.response: Considered the architectural concern. The architectural recommendation in EXP-401 is that Part 6.14 carries the *closure* of the κ argument (forward-magnetic to gravity arithmetic, defender-rescue closures, SG noise floor analysis, principled scope-condition options). The P3 paragraph adds an *attack surface* that is specific to WIN-012's role — surfacing the dome's own internal labeling inconsistency as a deep-attack vector. This is the WIN-012 entry point's natural job: open the case with the dome's own contradiction, then route to Part 6.14 for the structural closure. The two cross-references (WIN-058b, WIN-070) are short anchor links, not duplicate prose. The net WIN-012 length post-patches is ~4900 chars — well below the pre-compression 12,877 baseline and slightly above the post-EXP-401 4,339. The architectural intent (WIN-012 as brief, Part 6.14 as deep) is preserved.

kernel_of_truth.description: The kernel here is genuinely interesting: the BOU magnetometer at Boulder, Colorado registers ~−10.9 nT excursions under two physically distinct driving conditions (October 2003 G5 storm, with ring-current intensification; March 2017 partial eclipse, with ionospheric photoionization perturbation). Same instrument, same site, same numerical signature, different physics. If the dome had identified this as a structural property of the Boulder location (e.g., a local geomagnetic latitude × instrumental impedance × diurnal-conductivity coincidence), that would be a real observation worth modeling — the kind of empirical pattern that motivates physical theorizing.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome does not treat the coincidence as a Boulder-location property; it treats it as evidence for a universal coupling constant κ linking magnetic and gravity perturbations across all driving regimes and locations. That promotion requires (a) the gravity-side observation to be independently replicated (Mohe 1997 −6.5 μGal is not, per WIN-011), (b) the coupling to actually predict measurements at other sites and other times (it doesn't — WIN-013 Membach 1999 and WIN-014 China 2009 SG nulls falsify it), and (c) a derivation from the dome's primitives that explains why this number is what it is (κ = f(disc_radius, firmament_height, ...) is nowhere published). The kernel — same BOU excursion magnitude in two regimes — is at most an interesting Boulder-station fact; it is not a coupling constant. The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart silently treats the 2003 storm correlation as κ-direct-proof, while the live-power tile silently treats the 2017 eclipse correlation as κ-direct-proof. The two cannot both be 'direct proof' of the same constant unless the constant exists independently of either event — which is what a derivation from disc geometry would have to provide.

Part 6.14 ('How κ was derived' h3) — receives the P4 cross-reference back to WIN-012's inconsistency surface.
unaddressed_holes_note: EXP-430 scope is strictly WIN-012.c7 Hole 2 (moderate) / ISS-2156. Other c7 holes status: Hole 1 (TLDR factual error, major) was already addressed in a prior expansion (current tldr_evidence reads correctly per pre-run check — 'BOU magnetometer ... Mohe, China — two different sites, different instruments, different events'); Hole 3 (missing WIN-070 cross-reference, moderate) is already present in the current Cross-references paragraph ('For the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart claiming to show κ via GRACE L1A residuals ... see WIN-070'); Holes 4–6 (minor) — optional inline arithmetic and Yang & Wang citation re-additions, and PRED-TIER-2/3 annotation — are not addressed here and remain candidates for a low-priority polish EXP. The Part 6.14 'How κ was derived' h3 contains a duplicated paragraph ('The Mansinha 2001 reduction pipeline...' appears twice in the 'What past SG data actually shows' h3 per the section snippet inspection — note this is in 'What past SG data actually shows', NOT in 'How κ was derived'); flagging for the decider as a separate observation but explicitly NOT touched by this EXP.
notes_for_decider: Multi-file patch (data/wins.json + data/sections.json). Execution order: P1 → P2 → P3 within WIN-012, then P4 in Part 6.14. All four BEFORE strings verified anchor-unique (count=1) at authoring time. WIN-012 uses HTML entities (−, —, µ, κ, ", –); Part 6.14 uses literal Unicode (−, —, μ, κ, ", –). Patch text follows the destination file's existing convention in each case. After integration: (a) run tests/ (will validate counts/anchors and any cross-page consistency tests touching WIN-012); (b) verify the in-page anchor #win012 in Part 6.14 resolves (it should, as WIN-012 has id='012' which the build pipeline maps to id='win012' per existing usage); (c) no new outbound URLs — both cross-references (P3 to #win070, #win058b; P4 to #win012) are in-page anchors. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged. TLDRs unchanged. Detail_verdict unchanged. Code_analysis unchanged. Finding unchanged. After all four patches: WIN-012 reads consistently with Part 6.14 and the dome's own live-power tile on the κ-numerator attribution, and the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart is surfaced as an internal inconsistency rather than ignored.
deeper_analysis:
Post-EXP-401 the WIN-012 architecture (brief entry, Part 6.14 deep) was a net win, but it preserved the 2003-storm-attribution from the pre-compression text while Part 6.14 was built with the 2017-eclipse-attribution. The two pages therefore disagreed on a load-bearing fact (which event provides κ's numerator) — and the disagreement was inherited from the dome itself, which carries the same internal conflict between its live-power tile (2017 eclipse) and its home-page Forensic Zoom chart (2003 storm). The c7 curmudgeon review's stronger_arguments item #1 makes the structural observation: 'The dome cannot have it both ways: either the −10.9 nT is the 2017 eclipse (in which case the storm-context r = 0.992 of WIN-058b is a separate, unconnected coupling that the dome has nonetheless labeled with the same kappa symbol — a category error), or the −10.9 nT is the 2003 storm (in which case kappa is a storm-magnetic / eclipse-gravity ratio, not a magnetic-gravity coupling at all — different physical regimes). Either reading destroys WIN-012 as a two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant confirmation.'

The EXP implements the curmudgeon's preferred closure (option a) and adds the stronger_arguments #1 attack as an explicit paragraph in WIN-012, with a back-reference from Part 6.14. The result is rhetorically stronger than either page in isolation: WIN-012 is the entry point that surfaces the dome's contradiction; Part 6.14 carries the empirical-test closure (1999/2009 SG nulls falsify κ at the eclipses it predicts to apply to); the cross-reference (P4) routes readers from Part 6.14 back to WIN-012 if they want the contradiction-surface argument.

Considered the other side (per user preference): a defender could argue the 'two independent eclipse events' claim is a strawman because the dome's wins-page (raw-text/02-wins.txt:87) literally reads 'Source: BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997' — both eclipses, both at different sites, just summed as the κ definition. We are not strawmanning; we are pointing out that this self-description is inconsistent with the Forensic Zoom chart (which uses the 2003 storm), and the dome itself sums '2017 + 1997' to a ratio that the same number (−10.9 nT) is also used in a 2003-storm context on a different dome page. The defender's strongest response is 'fix the home-page chart to use the 2017 eclipse timestamps, then no inconsistency.' That would be a real responsive change — and is precisely the canary-style fix we want to surface, because it would force the dome to either republish the κ-direct-proof exhibit with eclipse timestamps (which removes the r = 0.992 storm correlation as a κ proof) or to maintain WIN-058b's storm-corollary framing and accept that the home-page Forensic Zoom chart is misattributed.

A second meta-observation for the decider: the dome's three different κ-numerator labels across its own pages (live-power: 2017 eclipse; Forensic Zoom: 2003 storm; wins-page: 2017 + 1997) are the kind of mid-revision artifact you get when an AI author iteratively builds a model and updates some pages without others. This is structurally similar to the 'TLDR factual error survived because operator-direct compression bypassed adversarial review' meta-observation in WIN-012.c7 — both are detectable only by cross-page audit, not by within-page review. The detector in our pipeline is the curmudgeon's cross_win_consistency check; the production fix would be a dome-side cross-page consistency audit, which the dome does not run.
tldr_change: None. tldr_evidence currently reads 'The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling constant divides a magnetic anomaly measured at Boulder, Colorado (BOU magnetometer) by a gravity anomaly measured at Mohe, China — two different sites, different instruments, different events. The Mohe gravity result has not been independently replicated; later superconducting gravimeter measurements at multiple sites found zero gravity signal, which would make this ratio undefined.' This is accurate under the new 2017-eclipse attribution (the TLDR does not specify a year, only 'different events'). tldr_verdict is also unchanged. Per the analyst TLDR-review standard: fix factual errors; don't split hairs on nuance. The TLDR is already factually correct after Hole 1's earlier repair; this EXP does not need to touch it.
